title: Fluffy Charred Canadian Tofu Casserole
description: A comforting Canadian dish featuring herbes-de-provence and charred tofu, this casserole is a jain-friendly delight that is both fluffy and savory.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium high heat and char the tofu until golden brown.
In a separate pan, saute the onion and garlic until the onion is translucent.
Add the mushrooms to the pan and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, herbes-de-provence, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Gradually add the vegetable broth and soy milk to the bowl, whisking until smooth.
Add the cooked mushroom mixture and charred tofu to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and bake for 25 minutes or until the casserole is set and lightly browned.
